text.title: Project Integration Management — Framework and Activities
text.summary: The PM-as-integrator model, the six integration activities, charter elements, and integrated change control process.

section.title: Integration Role

rule.statement: The PM acts as the cross-area integrator: every decision in one knowledge area must be assessed for impact on all others before approval.
rule.rationale: Silo management optimizes parts and sub-optimizes the whole. A scope CR approved without schedule and cost update creates a multi-week cascade of drift that is invisible until delivery fails.

section.p: Integration coordinates: Scope, Schedule, Cost, Quality, Risk, Resources, Communications, Procurement. Each has a subsidiary plan; the integrated plan references all of them from a single YAML source-of-truth.

list.item: Project Charter (Initiation) — authorize project, define high-level scope, bind sponsor commitment. Charter as code: sponsor approves via PR review.
Project Plan (Planning) — integrate all subsidiary plans into one YAML referencing scope (WBS), schedule, cost, risks, quality, resources, comms, procurement baselines.
Direct Work (Execution) — lead execution; ensure the plan governs, not informal decisions.
Monitor and Control (Throughout) — track against integrated baselines; emit GREEN/YELLOW/RED from numeric thresholds, not PM narrative.
Integrated Change Control (Throughout) — every CR triggers cross-area impact analysis before approval; decisions logged in decisions/ per ADR pattern.
Close Project (Closure) — formal acceptance, lessons learned, archive, contract closeout. Closure is integration's last mile — do not let the team scatter before it completes.

section.title: Charter Elements

rule.statement: Every SMART objective in the charter must cite a measurable success criterion; unknowns must be listed as TO_CONFIRM, not omitted.
rule.rationale: Charter generators hallucinate constraints and assumptions to appear thorough. Forcing unknowns to surface as TO_CONFIRM creates a tracked open-question list.

section.reference: Project charter template with approval block

section.title: Integrated Change Control

rule.statement: Re-baseline only when scope/budget/contract changes — not when velocity slips. Re-baselining for slip masks chronic delivery problems.
rule.rationale: Baseline changes are decisions, not corrections. Treating slip as a reason to re-baseline hides the root cause and makes EVM meaningless.

rule.statement: Every approved CR requires a cross-area impact memo committed alongside the CR before implementation begins.
rule.rationale: Second-order effects (a 2-week delay shifting dependencies onto a holiday, breaking a vendor deadline) only surface when impact is computed across all areas — not as a delta on duration alone.

antipattern.description: Status colour declared GREEN/YELLOW/RED based on PM narrative rather than numeric threshold comparison.
antipattern.reason: Agents over-summarize: declaring "GREEN — on track" without numeric backing is the dominant integration failure. Colour must follow threshold arithmetic.

section.reference: Script: compute status colours from numeric YAML inputs
